title: 10 best herbal teas for puffy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with puffy eyes because its unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenes reduces inflammation and congestion in the delicate skin around the eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe swollen tissues, while its astringent nature constricts blood vessels to minimize puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, yarrow's natural diuretic properties help to flush out excess fluids and toxins that can cause eye puffiness, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the skin around the eyes, thus alleviating puffiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in yarrow tea help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to puffy eyes, promoting a more healthy and radiant appearance.
Vasoconstrictive effects: Yarrow tea's vasoconstrictive properties help reduce blood flow to the area around the eyes, which can help alleviate puffiness caused by fluid retention or sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation, making it effective in relieving puffiness.
Leaves: Contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce water retention.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried yarrow flowers or leaves from a trusted source.
Heat one cup of boiling water in a teapot or kettle for three minutes.
Add the yarrow material to the hot water and let it steep for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down before applying it to the affected eyes as an eyewash, or drink it slowly to internal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and congestion. The herbal tea's active compounds, berberine and hydrastis, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to shrink swollen blood vessels and decrease fluid retention around the eyes. Additionally, goldenseal tea's ability to soothe and calm the skin can help to alleviate puffiness and dark circles, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A natural antibacterial agent that helps reduce puffiness by reducing inflammation and swelling caused by sinus infections or allergies.
Berbamine: An alkaloid that has a vasoconstrictive effect, which means it helps constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Sitosterol: A natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and redness by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain a high concentration of berberine, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness.
Barks: The barks of the goldenseal plant have been traditionally used to treat eye conditions, including puffiness, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder and one cup of boiling water.
Steep the goldenseal root powder in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or mug.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and mix well to combine the ingredients.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before using it as an eye compress to reduce puffiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with puffy eyes because its active ingredient, calendula, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin around the eye area. The tea's flavonoids also help to tighten blood vessels, diminishing dark circles and puffiness. As a result, pot marigold tea can provide immediate relief from tired, puffy eyes, leaving them looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and ease puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.

Soothing and anti-allergic effects: Pot marigold's soothing and anti-allergic properties help to calm and reduce irritation in the eye area, reducing redness and puffiness associated with allergies or other irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and soothing irritated eye areas.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the morning after dew has dried and rinse them gently with filtered water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or two teaspoons of fresh petals with one cup boiling distilled water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes depending on desired strength of tea.
Strain the tea into a clean glass using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before applying it with a cotton pad or cloth to reduce puffiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with puffy eyes because of its natural diuretic properties, which help to reduce water retention and alleviate swelling. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also work to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, dandelion tea's antioxidants and vitamins A and K can help to improve circulation and promote healthy skin elasticity, further reducing the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Dandelion tea's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness by tightening the skin around the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate eye irritation that may contribute to puffiness.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea's diuretic effects can help flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can contribute to puffy eyes by reducing water retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and soothe puffiness.
Leaves: High in vitamin A and potassium, which help reduce water retention and ease eye pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Rinse 2 tablespoons of dried or 4-6 teaspoons of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers with cold water.
Steep the rinsed herbs in one cup boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a large bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool and refrigerate it for at least 30 minutes before using it as an eye compress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique properties that reduce inflammation and ease eye strain. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols work to constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and swelling. Additionally, the calming effects of the tea can help to relax the muscles around the eyes, further alleviating discomfort. As a result, herbal purple coneflower teas provide a natural and soothing solution for those seeking relief from puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salvenic acid amide, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by combating inflammation.
Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.

Cicatrizant properties: Purple coneflower has been shown to have cicatrizant properties, meaning it can help promote wound healing and reduce scarring, which may be beneficial for reducing puffiness and discoloration under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling, making them effective in alleviating puffiness.
Buds: The buds of the purple coneflower contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea purpurea flowers at a ratio of one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried flower mixture into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before applying it to the affected eye area with a clean cotton pad.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with puffy eyes because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, it can help to reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness by reducing the body's natural response to inflammation. The antioxidants present in St john's wort also work to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to puffy eyes. Additionally, the calming effects of St john's wort may help to relax the delicate skin around the eyes, further reducing the appearance of puffiness and leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: St John's Wort tea contains a variety of antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce puffiness by neutralizing free radicals and soothing inflamed tissues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, may help to reduce swelling and alleviate redness associated with puffy eyes, promoting a more calm and refreshed appearance.

Vasodilatory effects: St John's Wort tea has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help to improve circulation and reduce fluid retention in the face and under-eye area, leading to a reduction in puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and anthraquinones that help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum flowers per cup of boiling water.
Pour the measured herb into a teapot or infuser and add one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve slowly.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before applying it as an eye compress for 5-10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with puffy eyes because its unique composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and essential oils work synergistically to reduce swelling and inflammation. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation and puffiness. Additionally, the tea's hydrating properties help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and looking smoother. Regular consumption of maidenhair tree teas can provide long-term benefits for a more radiant and youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea helps reduce puffiness by reducing inflammation and swelling in the eye area, allowing for a smoother and more even appearance.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the tea help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes and dark circles.
Constricting and toning properties: The tea helps constrict blood vessels and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness and promoting a smoother, more youthful look.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, relieving puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 dried ginkgo biloba leaves or 2 teaspoons of loose-leaf tea and store them in an airtight container.
Boil one cup of water for 5-7 minutes in a medium-sized saucepan, then reduce heat to low.
Add the dried ginkgo biloba leaves or loose-leaf tea to the boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, squeezing out any remaining liquid from the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that work together to reduce inflammation and fluid retention. The caffeine-free herbal tea is gentle on the delicate skin around the eyes, allowing it to penetrate deep and target the root causes of puffiness. As the soothing properties take effect, they help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, leaving the eyes looking refreshed, revitalized, and radiant with a calming clarity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to puffy eyes by neutralizing free radicals.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anthocyanins present in blueberries have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids found in blueberry tea may help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce water retention and puffiness in the eye area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blueberry roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease puffiness in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the blueberry plant contain antioxidants that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh or dried vaccinium myrtillus berries for every 8 oz of boiling water needed.
Combine the harvested blueberries and 1 cup of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the antioxidant-rich compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and achieve a clear liquid.
Chill the tea in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving to enhance its soothing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blackcurrant teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and vitamins that work together to reduce inflammation and swelling. The tea's anthocyanins, powerful plant-based compounds, help to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes. Additionally, blackcurrant tea's antioxidant properties soothe and calm irritated skin, while its vitamin C content boosts collagen production, leaving your eyes looking smooth, refreshed, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blackcurrant tea is rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to puffiness under the eyes.
Vasodilatory compounds: The tea contains flavonoids and anthocyanins, which dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing swelling and discoloration associated with puffy eyes.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Blackcurrant tea contains anti-inflammatory agents like quercetin and kaempferol, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness, redness, and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, easing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried blackcurrant leaves and flowers into a tea infuser.
Boil 250ml of water in a kettle or saucepan for 5 minutes.
Add the tea infuser to the boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Enjoy your blackcurrant tea immediately, allowing the antioxidants and flavonoids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique properties that promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and enhance blood circulation. The soothing extracts from the beach rose flowers calm and decongest the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the tea help to combat free radicals that can cause eye fatigue and discoloration, leaving the eyes looking brighter and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Beach rose tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in beach rose tea can neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye fatigue, dark circles, and puffiness, promoting healthy and youthful-looking eyes.
Vasodilatory properties: Beach rose tea's vasodilatory effects can help relax the blood vessels around the eyes, reducing congestion and swelling that may be contributing to puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation, alleviating puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of dried rosa rugosa flowers from your garden or local health food store.
Combine the dried flowers with one quart of boiling water in a large glass teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes then strain it into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add two tablespoons of chilled filtered water and refrigerate the tea for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to your closed eyes as an compress for 10-15 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for puffy eyes are a natural and soothing way to reduce swelling and discoloration caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or lack of sleep. These teas work by constricting blood vessels, reducing inflammation, and promoting circulation, which helps to alleviate puffiness. Examples of herbal teas that help with puffy eyes include peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root tea. By drinking these teas, individuals can enjoy clearer, brighter eyes that look refreshed and rejuvenated, improving their overall confidence and quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal teas is to brew a soothing cup of chamomile tea. This calming herb reduces inflammation and relaxes the skin around the eyes. Alternatively, peppermint tea cools and decongests the area, while green tea rich in antioxidants helps reduce puffiness. Simply steep the tea bag in hot water, let it cool, and gently place it over your closed eyes to reap the benefits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal teas are dark circles, sinusitis, and headaches. Peppermint tea helps to reduce puffiness by increasing blood flow and relaxing the skin. Echinacea tea can alleviate sinus pressure and congestion, reducing the appearance of puffiness around the eyes. Chamomile tea can also be used to treat headaches and promote relaxation, which can help to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes.
